segments.text: These are sound waves putting out a fire and they're being used to protect homes. Fire needs three ingredients: fuel,
heat, oxygen. And sound waves are really just pressure waves in the air. And they can disrupt fire by pushing and pulling the oxygen molecules away from the fuel,
depriving it of that last ingredient.
People have already made really cool fire extinguishers using this science,
but this team is working on scaling up that tech without making it really loud.
They're focusing on lower frequency sound waves that travel farther and can't be heard by humans or pets. In addition to the system for homes,
they're working on a backpack version for firefighters. If you need more optimistic science and tech stories, subscribe.
